old_exact: IN THE SUPREME COURTSTATE OF NORTH DAKOTA
new_exact: IN THE SUPREME COURT STATE OF NORTH DAKOTA
source_quote: I N T H E S U P R E M E C O U R T STATE
evidence: The PDF rendered image clearly shows 'IN THE SUPREME COURT STATE' as two separate words. The pdftotext extraction renders this with inter-letter spacing as 'I N T H E S U P R E M E C O U R T STATE' due to the formatted header layout, but 'COURT' and 'STATE' are distinct words separated by a space in the source. The DB text incorrectly fuses them as 'COURTSTATE'. String verified to appear exactly once in DB text using byte-exact search.
note: This is a split_join error (fusion) where OCR/ingestion combined two adjacent words. The correction restores the space between 'COURT' and 'STATE'.

lue of human life, and (c) felony murder. The charge ultimately

N.D.C.C. § 12.1-16-01(1)(a)—"attempt[ing] to intentionally or knowingly cause the
death of another human being." Pemberton asserts because it is possible the jury
convicted him based on attempted murd
evidence: Authoritative PDF (pdftotext extraction) shows em-dash (—, U+2014) preceding the quotation mark after the statute section. The DB contains a hyphen (-) in this position, which is an OCR/ingestion error. Standard legal citation formatting uses an em-dash in this context.
note: This is the only substantive defect in the opinion. All other differences between DB and source (straight vs. curly quotes/apostrophes, whitespace, line breaks, page markers) are intentional per processing policy or expected from pdftotext output.

source_quote:
N.D.C.C. ch. 12.1-05: Justification—
Excuse—Affirmative Defenses, specifically section 12.1-05-01, states justifications
evidence: Source PDF uses em-dashes (U+2014, UTF-8 bytes e2 80 94) between statute chapter section labels, but DB ingestion converted them to hyphens (U+002D, byte 2d). This is a clear OCR/character conversion error. Verified byte-for-byte: DB has '4a 75 73 74 69 66 69 63 61 74 69 6f 6e 2d 45 78 63 75 73 65 2d' (hyphens) while source has '4a 75 73 74 69 66 69 63 61 74 69 6f 6e e2 80 94 0a 45 78 63 75 73 65 e2 80 94' (em-dashes). Occurs exactly once in opinion.

source_quote: I conclude that only while the sentence "is deferred"—during the term of probation—may the court record of conviction be pleaded and proved.
note: Two ASCII hyphens (U+002D) in DB should be em-dashes (U+2014) as rendered in original PDF. Appears in concurring opinion by Justice Tufte.
